chap.def: 小伙子、傢伙、龜裂、使皸裂、小伙子的人的人物、龜裂地 (裂)
chap.ex: The cold winter wind caused her sensitive skin to chap.

chaparral.ex: The mountain was covered in a thick and dense chaparral.

chapel.def: 小教堂、祈禱室的人物 (單數)
chapel.ex: They held a small funeral service in the cemetery chapel.

chaperon/chaperone.def: (社交)監護人、伴護、監護、伴護地 (護)
chaperon/chaperone.ex: The senior teacher managed to chaperone the school trip.

chapiter.ex: The ornate chapiter of the column was a work of fine art.

chaplain.ex: The prison chaplain offered counsel to all the inmates monthly.

chaplaincy.ex: He applied for the position of hospital chaplaincy yesterday.

chaplet.ex: The young bride wore a beautiful floral chaplet on her hair.

chapped.def: (皮膚)裂開的、皸裂的人的人 (或物) (裂)
chapped.ex: He applied lots of lip balm to his dry and chapped lips.

chapter.def: 章節、公會議、重要轉折點人物 (單數)
chapter.col: final chapter, chapter and verse (詳盡細節)
chapter.ex: I am looking forward to reading the final chapter tonight.

char.def: 燒焦、打雜、把...燒成炭、打雜地 (焦)
char.ex: The fire managed to char the outside of the old wooden house.

charabanc.def: 觀光大巴士 (舊用語)人物 (單數)
charabanc.ex: They went on a day trip to the sea in an old charabanc.

character.def: 性格、品質、角色、文字、特徵的人物 (性)
character.col: main character, strong character, chinese character, character building
character.ex: The main character in the novel is a brave young sailor.

characteristic.def: 特徵、特色、典型的的人物 (特)
characteristic.ex: A unique characteristic of this bird is its bright red tail.

characteristically.ex: He characteristically arrived fifteen minutes early for tea.

characterization.def: 描繪、描述、角色特徵刻劃的人物 (單數)
characterization.ex: The movie is famous for its brilliant characterization of the villain.

characterize.def: 描繪、具有...特徵、表現的人物、特徵地 (徵)
characterize.ex: The region is characterized by high mountains and deep valleys.

charade.def: 猜謎遊戲、偽裝的人的人物 (單數)
charade.ex: The peace talks were eventually revealed as a total charade.

charcoal.def: 木炭、炭筆、深灰色、碳烤的人物、木炭地 (炭)
charcoal.ex: The artist used some black charcoal to draw the portrait.

chard.def: 甜菜、葉用甜菜(菜類)人物 (單數)
chard.ex: Swiss chard is a very healthy green vegetable for a salad.

charge.def: 索價、控告、充電、衝鋒、負責、充電的人的人物、負責地 (控)
charge.col: in charge of, free of charge, take charge, press charges
charge.ex: The hotel receptionist will charge you for the extra night.

chargeable.def: 應負債的、可控告的人的人物
chargeable.ex: Driving under the influence is a heavy chargeable offense.

charged.def: 充滿的、帶電的、受控告的人的人 (或物)
charged.ex: The two leaders had a very emotionally charged meeting today.

charger.def: 充電器、(古)戰馬、衝鋒者的人物 (單數)
charger.ex: I need to buy a new mobile charger for my broken phone.

chariot.def: (古)二輪戰馬車、敞篷馬車的人人物 (單數)
chariot.ex: The Roman army used a heavy racing chariot in the games.

charioteer.ex: The skilled charioteer won the dangerous race three times.

charismatic.def: 有魅力的、神賜能力的的人物
charismatic.ex: The charismatic leader managed to win the hearts of kids.

charitable.def: 慈善的、仁慈的、寬大的的人物
charitable.ex: She donated lots of her old clothes to a charitable cause.

charitably.ex: The rich family behaved charitably towards the poor people.

charity.def: 慈善、施捨、慈善機構、博愛的人物 (單數)
charity.col: charity event, charity begins at home, sister of charity
charity.ex: Money from the local event went to a children's charity.

charlatan.def: 江湖郎中、騙子人物 (單數)
charlatan.ex: The medical charlatan was selling fake cures for cancer.

charlock.ex: Bright yellow charlocks were growing in the open wheat field.

charm.def: 魅力、符咒、飾物、迷住、咒語的人的人物、迷人地 (魅)
charm.col: lucky charm, works like a charm (靈驗), prince charming
charm.ex: The small mountain village has a lot of rustic charm tonight.

charmed.def: 著迷了的、受保護的人的人 (或物) (魅)
charmed.ex: He seems to lead a truly charmed life with no real worry.

charmer.def: 有魅力的人、玩蛇者的人物 (單數)
charmer.ex: The snake charmer played a long flute to the cobra today.

charming.def: 迷人的、有魅力的的人 (或物) (魅)
charming.ex: The local hosts were very charming and kind to all of us.

charmingly.ex: She smiled charmingly at the waiter as she ordered the tea.

charred.def: 燒焦了的的人的人 (或物) (焦)
charred.ex: Only the charred remains of the big building were visible.

charring.def: 燒焦、炭化、燒焦的人 (或物) (焦)
charring.ex: The heavy wood showed some deep surface charring from fire.
